attacking_style: Attacks on sustained, steep gradients (12-15%) at high altitude, typically 3-4km from summit finishes. Prefers multiple accelerations to wear down rivals rather than single explosive moves, but lacks punch for decisive late attacks under 1km.
team_tactics: Relies heavily on Movistar setting brutal early tempo on climbs to reduce group size, but becomes vulnerable when isolated. Brother Dayer provides loyalty-based support. Team often positions him defensively rather than offensively, requiring personal chasing when attacks go.
terrain_preference: Dominates high-altitude (7000ft+) climbs with sustained 12-15% gradients. Exposed on explosive punchy finishes, gravel super-steep ramps, and all time trial distances. Cobbles handled better than expected but remains liability.
vulnerability: Chronic time trial weakness (loses 2+ minutes regularly). Drops immediately on explosive accelerations and punchy gradients. Third-week fatigue evident across Grand Tours. Tactically passive when needing time, waiting for others to lead rather than imposing.
key_patterns: Attacks multiple times on sustained climbs but burns matches early, vulnerable in final meters (2019 Vuelta)
Immediately dropped when rivals accelerate explosively on punchy terrain, needs sustained gradients (5+ observations)
Time trial losses consistently 2+ minutes in Grand Tours, predictable GC vulnerability (6+ observations)
Third-week decline pattern across Grand Tours, loses form and recovery capacity late race (4 observations)
Strongest at altitude on 12-15% gradients, dominated Col du Portet/Terminillo victories
Tactically passive when needing time, waits for others to lead attacks (2018 Vuelta Lopez incident)
form_trajectory: negative: 2022 showed brief Renaissance (Tour 2nd page) but immediately cracked on HC Alpine stages. 2021-2022 pattern reveals diminished climbing power on steep ramps that previously suited him, with visible front wheel weaving on 12-13% gradients.
